choices: Runs the file in the current shell so KEY=value lines become variables
items.explain: source (or .) executes the file in the current shell, setting variables.

items.prompt: Which file is usually safe to commit?
choices: .env.example with documented keys and safe defaults
items.explain: Commit the example template; gitignore real .env / .env.local with secrets.

items.prompt: KEY=value lines should not have spaces around the equals sign.
items.explain: Spaces break simple KEY=value parsing when sourcing.

items.prompt: If make fails with TOOLS unset, a good first step is…
choices: Source or export the project env (TOOLS path) then retry
items.explain: Missing TOOLS is an env literacy issue—load the file before blaming design.
